description_long: This object is a short sword with a broad, straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a rounded point. The blade is metallic and has a smooth, reflective surface, suggesting it is made of steel or a similar metal. The handle is cylindrical, appearing to be made of wood with a dark brown finish, and is capped with a rounded metallic pommel. A simple rectangular crossguard separates the blade from the handle, providing basic hand protection. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or engravings. The proportions and construction suggest it is a functional weapon, likely inspired by historical gladius or short sword designs.
description: A short, broad, double-edged sword with a metallic blade, wooden handle, and simple crossguard. The design is functional and unadorned.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the broad, straight blade, the rectangular crossguard, and the cylindrical handle with a rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, emphasizing the sword's flatness and the thickness of the crossguard.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the blade, crossguard, and handle, with similar proportions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness of the blade and handle, with the same features as view 1.
attribute_blade_type: straight, double-edged, broad, short sword blade
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, wooden, straight, with a rounded metallic pommel
